question.text: As per Regulation 2(1)(c) of the SEBI PFUTP Regulations, 2003, which of the following is TRUE about the definition of fraud?

answer.text: Fraud excludes acts committed through an agent
Fraud includes acts committed by a person or by any other person with his connivance or by his agent, whether or not there is any wrongful gain or avoidance of loss
Fraud requires proof of wrongful gain or avoidance of loss
Fraud can only be committed by the person directly dealing in securities

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 2(1)(c) explicitly states fraud includes acts committed whether in a deceitful manner or by a person or by any other person with his connivance or by his agent, WHETHER OR NOT there is any wrongful gain or avoidance of any loss. **The Trap:** Many candidates assume fraud requires proof of wrongful gain or loss avoidance. The regulation specifically removes this requirement. **Cross-Exam Context:** The NISM Series VIII exam also covers the definition of fraud under PFUTP for derivatives market participants. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 'whether or not there is wrongful gain' clause is crucial - it means intent to defraud is sufficient even without actual profit.
